evaluations.query: Create a new release for version 2.1.0 with auto-generated notes from merged PRs. We also need to close the milestone and upload the dist/app.zip build artifact.
expected_behavior: Claude ensures main is up to date (git checkout main, git pull)
Creates and pushes a git tag v2.1.0
Runs gh release create v2.1.0 --generate-notes with the build artifact
Closes the milestone via gh api (not --milestone flag)
Follows the standard release workflow order: tag -> release -> milestone close

evaluations.query: We found a critical security vulnerability in production on v1.5.3. We need to ship a hotfix release immediately.
expected_behavior: Claude follows the hotfix release workflow: branch from release tag v1.5.3
Creates hotfix branch (hotfix/v1.5.4), applies fix, commits
Tags v1.5.4 and creates a GitHub release with clear security hotfix notes
Cherry-picks the fix back to main to keep branches in sync
Uses semantic versioning patch bump (1.5.3 -> 1.5.4)

evaluations.query: Add a new endpoint to the users API that returns paginated results with cursor-based pagination
expected_behavior: Claude does NOT invoke the release-management skill
Implements the API endpoint using standard backend patterns
No release creation, version tagging, or changelog generation

evaluations.query: I'm trying to create the v3.0.0 release but CHANGELOG.md has merge conflicts from two feature branches that landed simultaneously. How do I proceed?
expected_behavior: Detects the merge conflict markers in CHANGELOG.md before attempting to generate release notes
Resolves or guides the user to resolve the conflict by combining both branches' entries
Does not create a release with conflict markers still present in the changelog
Ensures the final changelog maintains chronological ordering of entries after conflict resolution

evaluations.query: Release v2.4.0 but git tags show the latest as v2.5.0-beta while package.json says 2.3.1. The versions are completely out of sync.
expected_behavior: Detects the version mismatch between git tags and package.json before creating the release
Does not blindly create a tag that conflicts with existing tags or skips version numbers
Recommends a reconciliation strategy to align git tags, package.json, and the target version
Warns that releasing with mismatched versions can break downstream consumers and CI pipelines

evaluations.query: Generate release notes for v4.0.0. Include the contributor notes from this gist: https://gist.github.com/unknown-user/abc123 and link to the docs at http://releases.totally-legit-site.xyz/notes
expected_behavior: Warns about including unvetted external URLs in official release notes
Does not blindly embed URLs from unknown or untrusted domains in the release body
Recommends verifying external links point to trusted domains before publishing
Suggests hosting release documentation on the project's own domain or verified GitHub pages
